sources_searched: Florida CFO Division of Insurance Fraud

cases.case_name: Operation Sledgehammer - Palm Beach/Miami-Dade Staged Accident Ring

source.publication: US Attorney's Office, Southern District of Florida

people.name: Multiple defendants (93 total charged, 57 federal)
people.charges: Mail fraud, conspiracy, health care fraud
people.outcome: Sentences of 8-11 years; court-ordered restitution of $11M+

scheme.description: Operation Sledgehammer I through VI investigated chiropractic clinics involved in staged accidents and false insurance claims. Between October 2006 and December 2012, defendants staged automobile accidents and submitted false claims through controlled chiropractic clinics.

red_flags: Pattern of similar claims across a ring
All occupants went to same medical provider
Excessive medical treatment for minor impact
Involvement of known fraud-associated providers
Multiple claims by same individual in short period
cases.notes: One of the largest staged accident schemes in Florida history. Six phases of investigation. 93 defendants charged total.

cases.case_name: West Michigan Staged Accident Conspiracy - Florida Residents

source.publication: US Attorney's Office, Western District of Michigan

scheme.description: Florida-based ring operated staged accident conspiracy in West Michigan from 2012 to 2015. Leaders in Tampa directed operations remotely, with a local manager recruiting individuals to stage accidents and submit fraudulent claims.

red_flags: Pattern of similar claims across a ring
Multiple claims by same individual in short period
Claimant difficult to reach or changed contact info
cases.notes: Cross-state operation managed from Florida targeting Michigan insurers.

cases.case_name: Miami Staged Accident Scheme - CFO Patronis 2023

people.name: Five individuals (names in press release)
people.charges: Staged Accident, Grand Theft, Insurance Fraud, Organized Scheme to Defraud

scheme.description: Five individuals were paid $1,000 each to participate in a staged automobile accident and receive treatment at local PIP clinics with intent to fraudulently bill insurance companies.

red_flags: All occupants went to same medical provider
Pattern of similar claims across a ring
Injuries inconsistent with damage
cases.notes: Participants each paid $1,000 as inducement.

cases.case_name: Miami Staged Accident Scheme - CFO Patronis 2024

scheme.description: Four individuals arrested in Miami staged accident scheme targeting PIP insurance.

people.name: Kalil Davis
people.charges: Insurance fraud, assault with deadly weapon

cases.case_name: Jacob McNabb - Bakersfield Serial Collision Fraud

people.name: Jacob McNabb
people.charges: 4 felony counts insurance fraud, 11 counts false documents, perjury, identity theft

vehicles.claimed_damage: Repeated collision damage to same areas

scheme.description: McNabb engineered approximately 40 collisions over six years, filing insurance claims while concealing pre-existing vehicle damage. He kept insurance money instead of repairing vehicles, then reclaimed same damage areas. Also sued innocent drivers in small claims court.

red_flags: Multiple claims by same individual in short period
Pre-existing damage claimed as new
Prior history of insurance claims
No police report or inconsistent police report
cases.notes: CHP noticed unusually high collision frequency. One of the longest sentences for individual staged collision fraud in CA.

cases.case_name: LA Organized Staged Collision Ring - Valle-Diaz, Retana, Gomez

scheme.description: Organized ring in Los Angeles staged auto collisions to collect insurance money. All three admitted to an enhancement of more than $100,000 in losses and were ordered to pay $343,883 in restitution.

red_flags: Pattern of similar claims across a ring
Multiple claims by same individual in short period

cases.case_name: Bradley Pierre - Largest NY No-Fault Insurance Fraud

people.name: Bradley Pierre
people.charges: Conspiracy to commit bribery, conspiracy to defraud IRS
people.outcome: 10 years prison, 3 years supervised release, $3.5M forfeiture, $1.5M restitution

scheme.type: No-fault insurance fraud / fraudulent medical billing
scheme.description: Pierre orchestrated the largest no-fault auto insurance fraud in New York history over 13 years (2008-2021). He controlled clinics (Veda Medical, Sky Medical, Sun Medical, Rutland Medical), convinced doctors to submit false claims, extracted $20M+ from clinic accounts, received $1M in pharmacy kickbacks, and directed patients to law firms including one operated by his wife.

red_flags: All occupants went to same medical provider
Involvement of known fraud-associated providers
Excessive medical treatment for minor impact
Pattern of similar claims across a ring
Multiple claims by same individual in short period
cases.notes: Largest no-fault insurance fraud in New York history. Also defrauded IRS of $1.5M in taxes.

cases.case_name: NY No-Fault Psychological Services Fraud - $10M Scheme

scheme.description: Four men charged with defrauding New York auto insurers by submitting more than $10 million in fraudulent claims for psychological services that were either not provided or not medically necessary.

red_flags: Excessive medical treatment for minor impact
Involvement of known fraud-associated providers
All occupants went to same medical provider

cases.case_name: Jordan Ford - Dallas $5M Wire Fraud Insurance Scheme

scheme.description: Ford recruited insurance company employees to steal clients' personal information from legitimate claims. He then impersonated clients to redirect insurance payments to accounts he controlled. Some employees lent him company laptops to authorize fraudulent payments. All 9 defendants pled guilty.

red_flags: Claimant difficult to reach or changed contact info
Pattern of similar claims across a ring
cases.notes: Insider threat case with corrupted insurance employees.

cases.case_name: Earlie Dickerson - Texas Chiropractic Recruitment Scheme

vehicles.actual_condition: Real accidents exploited for unnecessary treatment

scheme.description: Dickerson recruited individuals involved in auto accidents and sent them to chiropractic clinics for medically unnecessary treatments. Used fraudulent bills from those treatments to obtain settlement checks from auto insurance companies.

red_flags: All occupants went to same medical provider
Excessive medical treatment for minor impact
Involvement of known fraud-associated providers

cases.case_name: Operation Sideswipe - New Orleans Staged 18-Wheeler Crashes

people.name: Vanessa Motta
people.charges: Conspiracy to commit mail/wire fraud, mail fraud, obstruction of justice, witness tampering
people.outcome: Guilty on all counts; sentencing July 7, 2026

people.name: Jason Giles
people.charges: Conspiracy to commit mail/wire fraud, mail fraud, obstruction of justice, witness tampering
people.outcome: Guilty on all counts; sentencing July 14, 2026

scheme.description: Attorneys Motta and Giles paid 'slammers' to fill cars with passengers and intentionally crash into tractor trailers on New Orleans highways. They then filed personal injury lawsuits for real or faked injuries. The scheme began as early as 2011. 63 individuals charged total, 40+ guilty pleas.

red_flags: Pattern of similar claims across a ring
All occupants went to same medical provider
Witnesses are related to or associated with claimant
Accident occurred in known staging location
Multiple claims by same individual in short period
cases.notes: One of the largest staged accident schemes in US history. Led to Louisiana tort law reform. A witness was murdered. 63 total charged.

cases.case_name: William Mize - Washington Multimillion Dollar Fraud

scheme.description: Mize defrauded insurance companies of $6M+ by staging vehicle collisions, boat accidents, and home incidents from 2013-2018. Used stolen funds for luxury homes, vehicles, and boats. Fled after indictment in 2019, lived under false identities for 5 years before capture in Jacksonville, FL.

red_flags: Multiple claims by same individual in short period
Vehicle recently purchased or with inflated value
Claimant difficult to reach or changed contact info
cases.notes: Fled authorities for 5 years, captured trying to sell a yacht under an alias.

Pre-existing damage claimed as new

cases.case_name: Christopher Brown - Cordova TN $5.9M Multi-Insurer Fraud
